title: SPLIT Psychological aspects of NGE vs Q Final
display_title: SPLIT Psychological aspects of NGE vs Q Final
wikitext:
[]: [SPLIT] Psychological aspects of NGE vs Q/Final. Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:34 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Psychology and mindfucks (which really kicked in during the series second half) is what helped make Eva, Eva. Q or Final will probably at least have ONE mindfuck or psychological sequence.

[]: Post by Legendary » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:38 am SJCrew wrote: The psychology and mindfucks are easily the most uninteresting parts of the series. Evangelion is revered for its brilliant storyline, and compelling character development, not its ability to confuse you.

[]: Post by Lucretius » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:42 am SJCrew wrote: Evangelion is revered for its brilliant storyline

[]: Post by Nonoriri » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:51 am The plot of Evangelion is easily the worst part.

[]: Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 3:08 am Legendary wrote: The mindfucks and psychology are what develop the characters. Go watch episode 22' again. The whole thing is like half an hour of brilliance. And that's not even touching on 16, early 19, 20, 23', 25, or EITHER 26.

[]: Post by Smugleaf » Sun Jun 27, 2010 7:25 am Nonoriri wrote: The plot of Evangelion is easily the worst part.

[]: Post by Halicat » Sun Jun 27, 2010 8:47 am Legendary wrote: The mindfucks and psychology are what develop the characters. Go watch episode 22' again. The whole thing is like half an hour of brilliance. And that's not even touching on 16, early 19, 20, 23', 25, or EITHER 26.

[]: Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 27, 2010 11:05 am Legendary wrote: The mindfucks and psychology are what develop the characters. Go watch episode 22' again. The whole thing is like half an hour of brilliance. And that's not even touching on 16, early 19, 20, 23', 25, or EITHER 26.

[]: Post by ran1 » Sun Jun 27, 2010 12:08 pm SJCrew wrote: No, I'm pretty sure it's all the major traumatic events occurring that develop the characters, not the random flashes of incoherent images that basically bore you for 5-10 minutes (or in 25 and 26's case, 20+ minutes).

[]: Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 27, 2010 12:11 pm ran1 wrote: I lol'd and I lol'd and I couldn't stop loling because my lolmeter was lol'd off the charts and then I lol'd some more. "RANDOM FLASHES OF INCOHERENT IMAGES" LOLWUT Since this is a Rebuild thread and I don't feel like posting my as of yet unfinished Episode 25 montage-subversion analysis, all I'm going to say is that I heartily disagree with this statement and advise you to watch the episodes again with a clear mind.

[]: Post by Halicat » Sun Jun 27, 2010 12:20 pm ran1 wrote: I don't feel like posting my as of yet unfinished Episode 25 montage-subversion analysis.

[]: Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:07 pm ran1 wrote: I lol'd and I lol'd and I couldn't stop loling because my lolmeter was lol'd off the charts and then I lol'd some more. "RANDOM FLASHES OF INCOHERENT IMAGES" LOLWUT Since this is a Rebuild thread and I don't feel like posting my as of yet unfinished Episode 25 montage-subversion analysis, all I'm going to say is that I heartily disagree with this statement and advise you to watch the episodes again with a clear mind.

[]: Post by Legendary » Sun Jun 27, 2010 1:24 pm SJCrew wrote: No, I'm pretty sure it's all the major traumatic events occurring that develop the characters...

[]: Post by SJCrew » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:32 pm You might not like EoTV

[]: Post by Lucretius » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:34 pm For me, the introspective scenes and the manner in which they were presented made the show. Take out the stick figure montages and sarcastic use of music and you've got something pretty banal.

[]: Post by Halicat » Sun Jun 27, 2010 2:43 pm Lucretius wrote: For me, the introspective scenes and the manner in which they were presented made the show. Take out the stick figure montages and sarcastic use of music and you've got something pretty banal.

[]: Post by sephirotic » Sun Jun 27, 2010 5:13 pm SJCrew wrote: FOR THOSE OF WHO WHO DIDN'T READ I DON'T WANT ANY OF THAT PSYCHOLGICAL CRAP IN Q, EVANGELION IS BETTER OFF WITHOUT IT

[]: Post by Oz » Sun Jun 27, 2010 5:38 pm SJCrew wrote: No, episodes 25 and 26 sucked.

[]: Post by SaikiEva » Sun Jun 27, 2010 7:32 pm SJCrew wrote: FOR THOSE OF WHO WHO DIDN'T READ I DON'T WANT ANY OF THAT PSYCHOLGICAL CRAP IN Q, EVANGELION IS BETTER OFF WITHOUT IT

[]: Post by NAveryW » Sun Jun 27, 2010 7:57 pm SJCrew wrote: Wrong. I love EoTV. It was my favorite anime for the longest of times, right up until I watched RahXephon.
